run.timeframe_label: 72h (strict window yielded 6 items; no expansion needed)

items.title: Anthropic Reverses Agent SDK Billing Change on Implementation Day
items.summary: Anthropic halted the Agent SDK billing restructuring on June 15 — the same day it was scheduled to take effect. The Help Center notice reads: 'We're pausing the changes to Claude Agent SDK usage described below. For now, nothing has changed.' Agent SDK, claude -p, and third-party app usage continue drawing from existing subscription limits. The previously described credit tiers ($20 Pro, $100 Max 5x, $200 Max 20x at API list rates) are not active. Anthropic committed to providing advance notice before any future change.

items.title: Microsoft Work IQ APIs Reach General Availability
items.summary: Three Work IQ endpoints reached GA on June 16: Agent-to-Agent (A2A) API, redesigned remote MCP server, and a REST API for web applications. Work IQ provides AI agents with permission-aware access to Microsoft 365 organizational data via a Rego-based policy engine. Usage is consumption-based, billed in Copilot Credits independent of a Microsoft 365 Copilot license. Vendor scenario estimates: $0.20–$1.50 per interaction. Three production deployments documented at GA: SLB, HP, Miro.
caveats: Pricing estimates are vendor-provided scenario models, not live billing data
Production implementations at GA are early-access customers; may not represent typical deployments

items.title: Claude Code Ships Permission Rule Syntax and Sub-agent Governance Fixes (2.1.178/2.1.179)
items.summary: Version 2.1.178 (June 15) added Tool(param:value) syntax to permission rules, nested .claude/ directory precedence, and sub-agent spawn classifier. Version 2.1.179 (June 16) fixed mid-stream connection drops, WSL2 mouse-wheel scrolling regression (introduced in 2.1.172), and denyRead/allowRead glob bug over large directory trees.

items.title: OpenAI Launches Partner Network with $150M Investment
items.summary: OpenAI launched its first formal partner program on June 14, backed by $150M in ecosystem investment. Three tiers: Select, Advanced, Elite. Specializations in Codex, cybersecurity, and AI agents. Forward Deployed Experts program for complex enterprise implementations. Target: 300,000 certified consultants by end of 2026. Colleen Kapase (former Google Cloud channel exec) hired to lead.
items.notes: openai.com returned 403 in this environment; key details confirmed via ChannelInsider (T3) and TechBriefly (T3). Tier set to T2 based on corroborating detail; downgrade to T3 if direct primary fetch fails.

items.title: GLM-5.2 Receives Independent Benchmark Coverage Four Days After Launch
items.summary: Z.ai launched GLM-5.2 on June 13 — 744B-parameter MoE (40B active), 1M-token context, MIT license, $12.60/month Coding Plan. VentureBeat published benchmark comparisons June 16 showing GLM-5.2 ahead of GPT-5.5 on SWE-bench Pro (62.1 vs 58.6) and FrontierSWE (74.4% vs 72.6%). BenchLM provisional: #3 of 124; verified leaderboard: #9 of 33.
caveats: Benchmarks are vendor-submitted leaderboard runs, not independently administered trials
Prior Zhipu models have shown large gaps between internal and independent benchmark results
Independent SWE-bench Verified and LiveCodeBench results not yet available

items.title: OpenAI Academy Adds Three Workplace AI Courses
items.summary: OpenAI Academy published three new courses on June 14: AI Foundations, Applied AI Foundations, and Agents and Workflows. Designed to take organizational teams from understanding AI basics to deploying agents in structured workflows.
items.notes: openai.com returned 403; confirmed via EdTech Innovation Hub and TechBriefly corroborating sources (T3).

items.title: Anthropic Technical Team Meets Commerce Department Over Fable 5 Ban
items.summary: Anthropic's Frontier Red Team held face-to-face meetings with the US Commerce Department's Center for AI Standards and Innovation on June 16, presenting a technical brief on Fable 5's cybersecurity infrastructure. Weekend calls involved Anthropic co-founder Tom Brown, Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick, and National Cyber Director Sean Cairncross. Anthropic characterizes the triggering jailbreak as affecting 'a small number of previously known, minor vulnerabilities'. No restoration timeline announced.
caveats: Meeting details sourced from press reporting only
No Anthropic primary statement covers the June 16 in-person sessions
Government position not confirmed from a US government primary source
